req.title: Bulk data export (audit)
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-091 mandates three test slices: (1) EP positive – an authorised bulk-client triggers /$export, receives 202 + Content-Location, polls to completion, and gets valid NDJSON with a matching AuditEvent; (2) NEG negative – an unauthorised caller receives 403 and no AuditEvent of type 'export-success' is emitted; (3) BVA boundary – a dataset whose size sits exactly at the pagination threshold is split across exactly two NDJSON pages, confirming the fence-post split and that an AuditEvent is emitted per page chunk. State-transition coverage (STC) is embedded in the positive case by walking the job through queued→in-progress→completed states. All tests are self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders; no shared mutable state.

tests.name: test_authorised_bulk_export_end_to_end
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client holds a valid Bearer token with bulk-export scope
At least one Patient resource exists in the test tenant
actions: POST /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async using authorised token
Assert HTTP 202 and capture Content-Location header
Poll Content-Location until status is 'completed' (max 30 s, 2 s interval)
Fetch each output URL listed in the completed manifest
Retrieve AuditEvents for this export operation
expected: Each output file is valid NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
At least one AuditEvent with outcome '0' and entity referencing the export job ID exists
trace: R-HLT-091: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest, time, json

def test_authorised_bulk_export_end_to_end(authorised_client, fhir_base_url, poll_job):
    resp = authorised_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={"Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}"
    content_location = resp.headers.get("Content-Location")
    assert content_location, "Content-Location header missing"

    manifest = poll_job(authorised_client, content_location, timeout=30, interval=2)
    assert manifest.get("status") == "completed"
    output_files = manifest.get("output", [])
    assert len(output_files) > 0, "No output files in manifest"

    for file_entry in output_files:
        dl = authorised_client.get(file_entry["url"])
        assert dl.status_code == 200
        for line in dl.text.strip().splitlines():
            json.loads(line)  # raises if not valid JSON

    job_id = content_location.split("/")[-1]
    audit_resp = authorised_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": job_id, "outcome": "0"},
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1, "Expected at least one success AuditEvent"

tests.name: test_unauthorised_bulk_export_returns_403
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client holds NO valid token (or a token lacking bulk-export scope)
actions: GET /$export with an invalid/missing Bearer token
Capture HTTP status code and response body
Query AuditEvents for any export-success event linked to this attempt
expected: Response body contains a FHIR OperationOutcome with severity 'error'
No AuditEvent with outcome '0' for this attempt exists
tests.executable:
import pytest, json, uuid

def test_unauthorised_bulk_export_returns_403(unauthorised_client, fhir_base_url):
    correlation = str(uuid.uuid4())
    resp = unauthorised_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={
            "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
            "X-Correlation-Id": correlation,
        },
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    severities = [i.get("severity") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert "error" in severities, "OperationOutcome must contain an error issue"

    # Verify no success AuditEvent was recorded for this correlation id
    audit_check = unauthorised_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": correlation, "outcome": "0"},
    )
    # A 401/403 here is acceptable; if reachable, total must be 0
    if audit_check.status_code == 200:
        assert audit_check.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "No success AuditEvent expected"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_pagination_at_threshold
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
Client holds a valid Bearer token with bulk-export scope
Test tenant has been seeded with exactly PAGE_SIZE Patient resources (fence-post boundary)
actions: Seed the tenant with exactly PAGE_SIZE Patient records via seed_patients fixture
Count the number of NDJSON output chunks returned in the manifest
Verify record count across all chunks equals PAGE_SIZE
Retrieve AuditEvents and count one per chunk
expected: Manifest contains exactly 2 output file entries (one full page + one boundary overflow page)
Total NDJSON records across all files equals PAGE_SIZE
An AuditEvent exists for each output file chunk (count >= 2)
trace: R-HLT-091: large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output
R-HLT-091: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest, json

PAGE_SIZE = 1000  # configured page size in fhir-lite

def test_bulk_export_pagination_at_threshold(
    authorised_client, fhir_base_url, poll_job, seed_patients, cleanup_patients
):
    patient_ids = seed_patients(authorised_client, fhir_base_url, count=PAGE_SIZE)

    resp = authorised_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={"Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
        params={"_type": "Patient"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    content_location = resp.headers["Content-Location"]
    job_id = content_location.split("/")[-1]

    manifest = poll_job(authorised_client, content_location, timeout=60, interval=3)
    assert manifest["status"] == "completed"
    output_files = manifest.get("output", [])
    assert len(output_files) == 2, f"Expected 2 chunks at boundary, got {len(output_files)}"

    total_records = 0
    for entry in output_files:
        dl = authorised_client.get(entry["url"])
        assert dl.status_code == 200
        lines = [l for l in dl.text.strip().splitlines() if l]
        for line in lines:
            json.loads(line)
        total_records += len(lines)
    assert total_records == PAGE_SIZE, f"Record count mismatch: {total_records}"

    audit_resp = authorised_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent", params={"entity": job_id}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert audit_resp.json().get("total", 0) >= 2, "Expected >=2 AuditEvents (one per chunk)"

    cleanup_patients(authorised_client, fhir_base_url, patient_ids)
